steps.title: Identify the decisive clue
steps.content: The sentence “But that doesn't mean that Johnson never learned what other people thought of her plays” pivots from the problem (professional barriers) to the solution. The blank must name how Johnson accomplished this learning.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B—”an opportunity for Johnson to get feedback on her plays”—directly fits.

The passage describes her gatherings where attendees “read and discuss one another's work, including Johnson's own,” creating exactly the feedback mechanism the blank requires.

The other three either describe something the passage contradicts (A: professional companies didn't support Black women's plays), reference content not discussed (C), or assert an unsupported inference (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
